data.title: Leo expands services with investments in Bevara Building Services and Blackfin, boosting national presence.
data.description: Leo, a network of expert facilities management service providers based in Pewaukee, Wisconsin, has recently formed partnerships with Bevara Building Services and Blackfin. Founded in 1996, Bevara is known for its dedicated on-site building engineers. Leo aims to expand its national facility service business by collaborating with top companies like Bevara and Blackfin, providing the necessary resources to drive their growth. The partnership signifies Leo's commitment to enhancing their services and expanding their reach in the industry.

data.title: Cushman & Wakefield reports strong financial results for 2025, with 34% EPS growth, $125M cash flow improvement, and $300M debt repayment.
data.description: In New York, Cushman & Wakefield Ltd. (NYSE: CWK) shared their financial performance for the last quarter and full year of 2025. CEO Michelle MacKay highlighted their achievements, noting a remarkable 34% growth in adjusted earnings per share. The company also saw a significant increase in cash flow exceeding $125 million and managed to reduce debt by prepaying $300 million. MacKay emphasized the positive state of commercial real estate markets, indicating strong market conditions.

data.title: Appian's Q4 earnings beat estimates by 400%, with EPS at $0.15. Revenue up by $36.18 million from last year.
data.description: On Thursday, February 19, 2026, at 07:05 AM, Appian (NASDAQ: APPN) released its Q4 earnings results. The company exceeded estimated earnings by 400.0%, posting an EPS of $0.15 compared to the expected $0.03. Revenue saw a significant increase, rising by $36.18 million from the previous year. This performance marks a positive trend as Appian continues to outperform projected financial figures. For further details, the complete story can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Jiuzi Holdings plans to raise $100 million at $2 per share from private investors to expand its Digital Asset Treasury strategy.
data.description: On February 19, 2026, Jiuzi Holdings, Inc. (Nasdaq: JZXN) disclosed that it had closed a $60 million initial strategic funding round on February 13, 2026. The company is currently in talks with various private investment entities to extend the private placement offering after the successful completion of the initial round. It is planning to raise an additional $100 million, with each share priced at a minimum of $2. This move aims to expedite the execution and growth of its Digital Asset Treasury (DAT) strategy.

data.title: Navan opens new Boston office to connect with Fortune 500 companies and startups, its sixth hub.
data.description: Navan, a leading global AI-powered business travel and expense platform listed on NASDAQ as NAVN, has revealed the establishment of a fresh office location in Boston, Massachusetts. This strategic decision aims to enhance face-to-face interactions and collaboration with numerous Fortune 500 companies and innovative startups based in the city. The company, dedicated to fostering personal connections, already boasts a significant presence in Boston with a considerable number of local clients. The Boston office marks Navan's sixth location in the Northern region, fortifying its commitment to expanding its reach and serving a diverse client base.

data.title: WELL Health and HEALWELL AI launch WELLTRUST platform to empower patients, AI in clinical research. TSX: WELL, OTCQX: WHTCF, TSX: AIDX, OTCQX: HWAIF.
data.description: In Vancouver and Toronto, WELL Health Technologies Corp. (TSX: WELL; OTCQX: WHTCF) and HEALWELL AI Inc. (“HEALWELL”) (TSX: AIDX) (OTCQX: HWAIF) have unveiled the initial stage of their collaboration on WELLTRUST™. This collaborative endeavor marks the partnership between a technology-driven healthcare firm operating outpatient clinics and a healthcare artificial intelligence company specializing in preventive care. WELLTRUST™ is a digital health platform focused on enhancing secure healthcare services.

data.title: Upbound Group, Inc. (NASDAQ:UPBD) reveals Q4 and FY2025 results, with earnings to be detailed in an investor call at 9am ET. Visit https://investor.upbound.com for information. CEO Fahmi Karam and CFO Hal Khouri to lead the review session.
data.description: Upbound Group, Inc. headquartered in Plano, Texas, disclosed its financial outcomes for the quarter and fiscal year concluding on December 31, 2025. The Company, traded on NASDAQ as UPBD, shared this information along with accompanying financial data on its investor relations site: https://investor.upbound.com. The CEO, Fahmi Karam, and CFO, Hal Khouri, will lead a conference call at 9 a.m. ET today to discuss the Company's financial performance.

data.title: Pool Corporation reported $5.3 billion in annual net sales for 2025, with a gross margin of 29.7% and diluted EPS of $10.85. They provided 2026 EPS guidance of $10.85 - $11.15. Operating expenses increased to $992.3 million in 2025. Net cash provided by operations was $365.9 million in 2025. In the fourth quarter of 2025, net sales decreased 1% to $982.2 million, with operating income decreasing 14% to $52.0 million. For 2026, they expect earnings in the range of $10.85 to $11.15 per diluted share.
data.description: POOL Corporation announced their full year and fourth quarter 2025 results, with net sales of $5.3 billion consistent with 2024. Gross margin improved to 29.7%, a 20 bps increase over the prior year. Adjusted diluted EPS for 2025 was $10.73. Operating expenses increased by 4% to $992.3 million, leading to operating income of $580.2 million (6% lower than 2024). Net income decreased to $406.4 million in 2025. For the fourth quarter, net sales decreased by 1% to $982.2 million, and gross margin improved to 30.1%. Operating income decreased to $52.0 million. Earnings per diluted share were $0.85 for the quarter. POOLCORP predicted 2026 earnings in the range of $10.85 to $11.15 per diluted share. Inventory balance grew 13% to $1.5 billion by December 31, 2025, and total debt outstanding increased to $1.2 billion. Net cash provided by operations was $365.9 million in 2025. Non-GAAP financial measures and forward-looking statements were also disclosed. POOLCORP operates as a wholesale distributor of swimming pool and related backyard products, aiming for sustainable growth and innovation while navigating various market challenges ahead.

data.title: Walmart raises annual dividend to $0.99 per share for fiscal year 2027, a 5% increase from the previous year.
data.description: The Board of Directors of Walmart Inc. (Nasdaq: WMT) based in Bentonville, Ark., has approved an annual cash dividend of $0.99 per share for the fiscal year 2027. This marks a 5 percent increase from the previous fiscal year's dividend of $0.94 per share. The dividend will be distributed in four quarterly installments of $0.2475 per share. The record dates for this dividend are March 20, 2026, and the payable dates are April 6, 2026, and May 8, 2026.
